title: Microsoft introduces Scout, an always-on personal agent for Microsoft 365
friendlyTitle: Microsoft Scout wants to handle work in the background
summary: Microsoft introduced Scout as its first ‘Autopilot’ agent: an always-on assistant intended to coordinate meetings, prepare materials, track deliverables and act within governed Microsoft 365 permissions.
friendlySummary: Scout is Microsoft’s new always-on agent for Microsoft 365. It is designed to help with meetings, preparation, follow-ups and task coordination.
whyItMatters: This is the clearest version of the shift from chatbots that answer questions to agents that carry work forward in the background. The useful promise is less coordination overhead; the key risk is whether users can supervise actions clearly.
friendlyWhyItMatters: If it works well, this kind of agent could reduce the busywork around calendars and coordination. It will need clear controls so people know what it is doing and why.
